mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which generates positive short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises, or the signal predictive power vanishes during periods of high volatility (VIX > 25).

mechanism_narrative: The coexistence of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both inflation and rates are elevated, or the interaction effect is dominated by single-series effects in stepwise regression.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently coincides with negative 60-day forward returns, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.0 percent signals severe economic deceleration and recession risk, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets rally despite sub-1% GDP growth, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a broad-based economic expansion, which boosts investor confidence and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when labor markets tighten alongside production gains, or the interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample validation.
